findings: CLI subcommands are prepare, inspect, and cleanup; merge is absent.
Every command emits one schemaVersion 1.0.0 JSON document with command, context, error, ok, operations, and state.
inspect reports a point-in-time branch, worktree path, HEAD, dirty changes, and lock state.
interpretations: The script can establish current worktree state but cannot establish or persist integration state against a target branch.
limitations: The source contract does not define the desired merge strategy or target branch.

findings: transition to done requires --human-review approved and writes approved status and approvedAt.
Review and done validation require execution, quality, AI review, report, and registered evidence, but do not require merge evidence.
The profile states that Human Review contains merge boundaries, while merge is not a Work Unit lifecycle status.
interpretations: Human approval and Git integration are orthogonal states in the current model.
limitations: Generic section content permits additional semantic objects, so schema validity alone does not establish a normalized integration receipt.

content.method: full package validation and current Git state inspection
findings: The Work Unit validates as done and Human-approved.
The registered evidence snapshot reports dirty true and headCommit b0f53e6ca9b2e332acba89b67de352904563b371.
Current inspect reports dirty false and headCommit b8ded97a179bbf6bc4c3b649fd5f9cffd3f6c263.
The source branch is an ancestor of main, proving integration, but the Work Unit has no merge receipt.
human-review content still says Pending while its attributes say approved.
interpretations: Historical execution snapshots and current live state are conflated, and approval content can drift from manager-owned attributes.
limitations: This is one recent execution case and does not cover conflict or failed-merge recovery.

content.method: commit and Work Unit artifact history inspection
findings: Earlier Work Units were integrated with two-parent merge commits ba5c7ed and 8e7bceb.
Post-merge Work Unit updates manually wrote fields such as mergeCommit, integratedMainHead, and mergeDecision into report content.
The recorded merge field shapes differ between Work Units and are not produced by a shared command.
interpretations: A fast-forward-only implementation would not cover all repository merge history, and manual free-form fields do not provide a stable viewer contract.
limitations: Historical commits show outcomes but not every preflight command or failure path.

findings: Content items have a stable id and kind but unrestricted content and attributes objects.
Work Unit lifecycle statuses are backlog, ready, working, review, done, and blocked; no merged status exists.
interpretations: Integration should be modeled as an orthogonal typed result or evidence record rather than adding a mutable merged lifecycle status without a broader lifecycle decision.
limitations: A normalized integration-result content contract is not currently specified.

interpretations: The analyzed prepare, inspect, cleanup, review, and done behavior is the currently passing baseline.
limitations: No merge or integration receipt tests exist because the capability is absent.

content.question: main에도 다른 변경이 생겼을 때 Work Unit branch를 어떤 전략으로 병합할 것인가?
content.rationale: 최근 fast-forward 사례와 기존 two-parent merge commit 사례를 모두 지원하면서 분기된 경우에만 Human 통제를 요구한다.
choices: B: FF 가능 시 단순 병합하고, 분기 시 명시적 Human 승인 후 no-ff 병합
C: 항상 no-ff merge commit 생성
